title: Architecture Canvas layout direction
summary: Choose how current and proposed topology preserve one stable spatial model.

sections.title: Primary skeleton · Exclusive view modes
sections.summary: Current and proposed states reuse one stable ELK layout and switch without moving shared nodes.

regions.title: State and scenario controls
regions.detail: Only one topology state is visually active at a time.

regions.title: Ownership and scenarios
regions.detail: Focus controls remain outside the graph camera.

regions.title: Stable proposed layout
regions.detail: Typed nodes, ports, edges, and nested ownership retain their positions across modes.

elements.text: Changed nodes are emphasized; unchanged topology stays spatially stable.

sections.title: Choose the Architecture Canvas state comparison
sections.summary: Both share one stable layout; they differ in simultaneous visibility.

options.label: Exclusive view modes
options.detail: Switch Current or Proposed in one full-width canvas while preserving shared node positions.
points: 9/10 · Maximum graph readability and camera space.
Comparison depends on stable positions and change emphasis.

options.label: Synced compare split sharing one layout
options.detail: Show Current and Proposed side by side with synchronized cameras and the same layout coordinates.
points: Each canvas loses width and dense graphs become harder to read.
